content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the success-reconciliation feature and found one high-severity recovery bug: reconciliation failures are currently unrecoverable because the code relies on `retry_task`, but retries on a completed run are rejected before reconciliation can rerun.

## Amendments

1. Fix reconciliation failure recovery in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`. The new logic assumes `retry_task` can re-run success reconciliation after `reconciliation_br_failed`, `reconciliation_metadata_error`, or `reconciliation_milestone_update_failed` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1608-1616`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1755-1762`), and it marks the daemon task failed/releases its lease on that path (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1496-1520`). But `retry_task` only moves the task back to `Pending` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:453-482`); the next dispatch rereads the project run snapshot (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2373-2380`) and immediately rejects `RunStatus::Completed` before any reconciliation code runs (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2958-3010`). After any transient reconciliation error, operators therefore get a permanent fail/retry/fail loop and the bead/milestone never gets reconciled. Add a dedicated reconciliation-only retry path, or reset/reopen the controller/run state so a retry can actually enter reconciliation.
   Rationale: This is a high-severity correctness gap: the advertised recovery path does not work for the very failures this handler introduces, so transient `br`/journal/milestone-store errors after a successful run can strand milestones indefinitely.
